free_text: Neutron activation cross sections measured at KRI
Data from initial publication are in Entries
Voltage 220-320 kV, the deuteron current 0.01-0.5 mA .
The samples were gathered into assemblies that were
mounted relative to the beam direction at the angles of
150 degrees (the ring No. 2 for eight assemblies). In
this arrangement, the neutron energy interval
Most of the standard sample assemblies:
Nb- sample X1 - Al - sample X2 -Nb or
Variations of the neutron flux during
irradiation were measured by two independent
Ge(Li) detector of 160 cm3 volume, energy
high-pure Ge detector with energy resolution
1.8 keV at Egamma 1332.5 keV and relative efficiency
were used for measurement of gamma radiation of
Contribution of scattered neutrons was minimized by the
use of thin-wall constructions of the target chamber
The target warm was withdrawn with a focused air jet.
The target-sample distance - 30 mm, beam diameter 5mm,
diameter of the sample 14 mm, sample thickness ~1 mm.
All data were revised with the latest reference
abundance of the target nuclei, half-lives and gamma
- correction factor responsible for the decay during
It's assumed that the uncertainty contributors are
The contribution of uncertainties of other parameters
is small and can be neglected.
